:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1438761681033-6461ffad8d80?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
27:["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["path",{"d":"m9 18 6-6-6-6","key":"mthhwq"}]],"className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}]

2a:["$","li","3",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":"$27:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","span",null,{"className":"text-stone-600 font-medium line-clamp-1","children":"Daily Planning: The System That Makes Every Workday Intentional"}]]}]
2b:["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 sm:p-8 mt-4 shadow-lg border border-stone-100 dark:border-stone-800 mb-10","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-bold text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 leading-tight mb-5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Daily Planning: The System That Makes Every Workday Intentional"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/authors/sara-osei","className":"flex items-center gap-2 group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1438761681033-6461ffad8d80?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Sara Osei","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"Sara Osei"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Productivity Researcher"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L6",null,{"ref":"$undefined","iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","7 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"March 31, 2026"}]]}]]}]
2c:["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"Most people start the workday without a plan and end it unsure what they actually accomplished. A daily planning practice changes this — not through rigid scheduling but through deliberate prioritisation. Here is the system that works."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"the-planning-paradox","children":"The Planning Paradox"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Most people do not plan their workday because planning feels like a distraction from the work itself. This is exactly backwards. The 10–15 minutes spent planning a workday typically returns 2–3 hours of effective time by eliminating the decision fatigue, reactive drift, and end-of-day uncertainty that unplanned days produce."}}],["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A workday without a plan defaults to whoever or whatever makes the most noise. Email, Slack, ad hoc requests, and the easiest items on the to-do list claim the hours. The important work gets deferred — again."}}],["$","h2","3",{"id":"the-mit-method","children":"The MIT Method"}],["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The foundation of any daily planning system is identifying the Most Important Tasks (MITs) — the specific things that, if completed, would make the day genuinely successful regardless of everything else that happened."}}],["$","p","5",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The rule: three MITs per day, maximum. No more."}}],["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Three seems too few. It is not. Most people who plan without constraints write eight to twelve tasks they intend to complete and finish the day having addressed urgent things that were on none of the lists."}}],["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Three MITs forces prioritisation. It makes the question \"what actually matters today?\" unavoidable. And it creates a realistic daily target that generates momentum and genuine satisfaction when met."}}],["$","h2","8",{"id":"the-night-before-principle","children":"The Night Before Principle"}],["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The single most effective improvement to a daily planning practice: do it the night before, not the morning of."}}],["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Planning in the morning competes with the morning's highest-quality cognitive state — the time most valuable for deep work. Planning the night before uses the end-of-day wind-down, closes open loops before sleep (reducing the background processing that causes poor sleep), and means you start the morning knowing exactly what to do."}}],["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The evening planning session: 10 minutes. Review what happened today. Write three MITs for tomorrow. Note any hard commitments (meetings, calls) that constrain tomorrow's schedule."}}],["$","h2","12",{"id":"time-blocking-integration","children":"Time Blocking Integration"}],["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The MIT method tells you what to do. Time blocking tells you when."}}],["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"After identifying your three MITs, assign each a time block in tomorrow's calendar. Treat these blocks as committed appointments. Schedule the most cognitively demanding MIT in your peak cognitive hours (typically mid-morning)."}}],"$L2d","$L2e","$L2f","$L30","$L31","$L32","$L33","$L34","$L35","$L36","$L37","$L38","$L39"]}],"$L3a","$L3b","$L3c"]}],"$L3d"]}]
3e:I[94097,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
3f:I[24170,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
40:I[55132,["/_next/static/chunks/0wjbooevnfpw4.js","/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js"],"default"]
:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1511671782779-c97d3d27a1d4?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
:HL["https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1545987796-200677ee1011?w=1200&h=800&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","image"]
2d:["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For most people this looks like:"}}]
2e:["$","ul","16",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"9:00–10:30: MIT 1 (deep work, no interruptions)"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"10:30–12:00: MIT 2 or meetings"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"13:00–14:30: MIT 3 or continued deep work"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"14:30–17:00: shallow work, email, administrative tasks"}}]]}]
2f:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For the full time blocking system, see our <a href=\"/blog/time-blocking-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">time blocking guide</a>."}}]
30:["$","h2","18",{"id":"the-weekly-context","children":"The Weekly Context"}]
31:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Daily planning works best within a weekly context. Every Monday morning (or Sunday evening), spend 20 minutes identifying the week's priorities — the three to five outcomes that would make the week successful."}}]
32:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Daily MITs then flow naturally from the weekly priorities rather than being chosen fresh each day without context."}}]
33:["$","h2","21",{"id":"the-shutdown-ritual","children":"The Shutdown Ritual"}]
34:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Close each workday with a consistent shutdown ritual:"}}]
35:["$","ol","23",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Review today's MIT list — what was completed, what was not"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Process any new captures into the appropriate list or folder"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Write tomorrow's three MITs"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Close all work applications and browser tabs"}}],["$","li","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Say or write \"shutdown complete\""}}]]}]
36:["$","p","24",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The last step sounds theatrical. It is not. The explicit declaration helps the brain release work-related processing, which measurably improves evening relaxation and sleep quality."}}]
37:["$","h2","25",{"id":"the-planning-journal","children":"The Planning Journal"}]
38:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Many high performers maintain a physical planning journal rather than digital tools. The act of handwriting MITs, notes, and reflections creates a different cognitive engagement than typing."}}]
39:["$","p","27",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A simple A5 notebook, one page per day, with three sections: MITs (top), schedule sketch (middle), end-of-day reflection (bottom). The physical format means reviewing previous days requires turning pages rather than clicking — a small friction that encourages more honest reflection on patterns over time."}}]
3a:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400","children":"Topic:"}],["$","$L7",null,{"href":"/blog?category=Productivity","className":"text-sm font-semibold px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-green-50 text-green-700 hover:opacity-80 transition-opacity","children":"Productivity"}],[["$","$L7","daily-planning",{"href":"/blog/tag/daily-planning","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","daily-planning"]}],["$","$L7","productivity",{"href":"/blog/tag/productivity","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","productivity"]}],["$","$L7","habits",{"href":"/blog/tag/habits","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","habits"]}],["$","$L7","time-management",{"href":"/blog/tag/time-management","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","time-management"]}],["$","$L7","focus",{"href":"/blog/tag/focus","className":"text-sm font-medium px-3 py-1 rounded-full bg-stone-100 dark:bg-stone-800 text-stone-600 dark:text-stone-300 hover:bg-orange-50 dark:hover:bg-orange-900/30 hover:text-orange-600 dark:hover:text-orange-400 transition-colors","children":["#","focus"]}]]]}]
3b:["$","$L3e",null,{"title":"Daily Planning: The System That Makes Every Workday Intentional","slug":"daily-planning-guide"}]
3c:["$","div",null,{"className":"print:hidden","children":["$","$L3f",null,{}]}]
3d:["$","aside",null,{"className":"space-y-5 print:hidden","children":[["$","$L40",null,{"items":[{"id":"the-planning-paradox","text":"The Planning Paradox","level":2},{"id":"the-mit-method","text":"The MIT Method","level":2},{"id":"the-night-before-principle","text":"The Night Before Principle","level":2},{"id":"time-blocking-integration","text":"Time Blocking Integration","level":2},{"id":"the-weekly-context","text":"The Weekly Context","level":2},{"id":"the-shutdown-ritual","text":"The Shutdown Ritual","level":2},{"id":"the-planning-journal","text":"The Planning Journal","level":2}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 border border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1438761681033-6461ffad8d80?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Sara Osei","className":"w-14 h-14 rounded-2xl object-cover object-top mb-4"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"font-bold text-lg text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 mb-0.5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Sara Osei"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-orange-500 font-medium mb-3","children":"Productivity Researcher"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-sm text-stone-500 dark:text-stone-400 leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Studies how physical and digital environments affect focus and output. 23:[["$","meta","0",{"charSet":"utf-8"}],["$","meta","1",{"name":"viewport","content":"width=device-width, initial-scale=1"}]]
42:I[27201,["/_next/static/chunks/01xlw8hd842-c.js","/_next/static/chunks/0d3shmwh5_nmn.js"],"IconMark"]
20:null
25:[["$","title","0",{"children":"Daily Planning: The System That Makes Every Workday Intentional"}],["$","meta","1",{"name":"description","content":"Most people start the workday without a plan and end it unsure what they actually accomplished. A daily planning practice changes this — not through rigid scheduling but through deliberate prioritisation. Here is the system that works."}],["$","link","2",{"rel":"manifest","href":"/manifest.webmanifest","crossOrigin":"$undefined"}],["$","link","3",{"rel":"canonical","href":"https://deskbly.com/blog/daily-planning-guide"}],["$","meta","4",{"property":"og:title","content":"Daily Planning: The System That Makes Every Workday Intentional"}],["$","meta","5",{"property":"og:description","content":"Most people start the workday without a plan and end it unsure what they actually accomplished. A daily planning practice changes this — not through rigid scheduling but through deliberate prioritisation. Here is the system that works."}],["$","meta","6",{"property":"og:url","content":"https://deskbly.com/blog/daily-planning-guide"}],["$","meta","7",{"property":"og:image","content":"https://deskbly.com/blog/daily-planning-guide/opengraph-image?9c5bff7ce8c4574f"}],["$","meta","8",{"property":"og:image:type","content":"image/png"}],["$","meta","9",{"property":"og:image:width","content":"1200"}],["$","meta","10",{"property":"og:image:height","content":"630"}],["$","meta","11",{"property":"og:type","content":"article"}],["$","meta","12",{"property":"article:published_time","content":"2026-03-31"}],["$","meta","13",{"property":"article:author","content":"Sara Osei"}],["$","meta","14",{"name":"twitter:card","content":"summary_large_image"}],["$","meta","15",{"name":"twitter:title","content":"Daily Planning: The System That Makes Every Workday Intentional"}],["$","meta","16",{"name":"twitter:description","content":"Most people start the workday without a plan and end it unsure what they actually accomplished. A daily planning practice changes this — not through rigid scheduling but through deliberate prioritisation. Here is the system that works."}],["$","meta","17",{"name":"twitter:image","content":"https://deskbly.com/blog/daily-planning-guide/opengraph-image?9c5bff7ce8c4574f"}],["$","meta","18",{"name":"twitter:image:type","content":"image/png"}],["$","meta","19",{"name":"twitter:image:width","content":"1200"}],["$","meta","20",{"name":"twitter:image:height","content":"630"}],["$","link","21",{"rel":"icon","href":"/icon?3236db0edf9ee0c4","alt":"$undefined","type":"image/png","sizes":"32x32"}],["$","link","22",{"rel":"apple-touch-icon","href":"/apple-icon?81494c1d6d9c49d0","alt":"$undefined","type":"image/png","sizes":"180x180"}],["$","$L42","23",{}]]
